heroCompanyDescription: West African Resources Limited (ASX: WAF) is an ASX-listed gold producer with its primary asset, the Sanbrado Gold Mine, located in Burkina Faso, West Africa. Sanbrado achieved commercial production in March 2021 and has established itself as one of the higher-grade open-pit and underground gold operations in the region, with the M19 underground deposit providing a high-grade ore source that underpins the mine's unit economics.

skew.rationale: The probability distribution is modestly skewed to the downside, with the bear case (N3 at 28%) carrying more weight than the bull case (N1 at 25%), reflecting the asymmetric and difficult-to-hedge nature of Burkina Faso geopolitical risk. While the gold price environment is highly supportive and Sanbrado is an established operation, the single-asset, single-jurisdiction concentration means a security or policy shock would have an outsized negative impact that is not fully offset by the upside scenarios available in the current environment.

verdict.text: West African Resources presents a high-risk, high-leverage gold exposure for investors willing to accept Burkina Faso jurisdiction risk in exchange for what has historically been a discounted entry point to a functioning, cash-generative gold mine. The gold price environment as of March 2026 is among the most supportive in recent history, with precious metals broadly in a strong uptrend as evidenced by silver's +7.1% single-session gain, and this tailwind is the primary near-term support for WAF's investment case. However, the absence of current financial data, production updates, and news flow at initiation means we cannot confirm whether Sanbrado is tracking to expectations, and the Burkina Faso security situation remains a non-trivial and asymmetric downside risk that warrants a higher return hurdle than peers. We initiate with a Neutral/High Risk stance, acknowledging the compelling gold price backdrop while flagging that the bear case probability of 28% reflects genuine and difficult-to-hedge geopolitical tail risk. The priority for this coverage is to obtain current production, cost, and financial data to build a full NAV model and refine the probability weightings.

featuredRationale: The four hypotheses sum to 100%, with the base case (N2, 40%) carrying the highest weight as expected for an initial coverage initiation on an established producer. The bear case (N3, 28%) outweighs the bull case (N1, 25%), reflecting the non-trivial and asymmetric downside from Burkina Faso security deterioration. The tail scenario (N4, 7%) captures low-probability M&A optionality in a strong gold price environment.

identity.overview: West African Resources Limited (ASX: WAF) — auto-added to coverage. Full company overview pending analyst research.

hypotheses.title: N1: Growth/Recovery
hypotheses.description: West African Resources successfully optimises the Sanbrado gold mine in Burkina Faso, delivering production at the upper end of guidance while advancing the M'pesso satellite deposit into production. A sustained gold price above US$2,800/oz combined with operational discipline drives free cash flow materially above consensus, triggering a valuation re-rating. This scenario requires stable in-country security conditions, disciplined capital allocation, and gold prices holding at current elevated levels.
supporting: Gold price environment is historically supportive, with spot near multi-year highs providing strong revenue tailwinds
Sanbrado has demonstrated it can produce at scale since achieving commercial production in 2021, with established infrastructure reducing execution risk
M'pesso satellite deposit provides a low-capital-intensity production growth option that could extend mine life and lift group output
WAF has historically traded at a discount to ASX gold peers due to jurisdiction risk, meaning any de-risking event could compress that discount sharply
contradicting: Burkina Faso security situation remains a material operational and reputational risk that caps the re-rating ceiling
No current financial data available to confirm whether free cash flow generation is tracking ahead of expectations
Elevated gold price may already be partially priced in by the market

hypotheses.title: N2: Base Case
hypotheses.description: WAF continues to operate Sanbrado at or near nameplate capacity, generating solid free cash flow in a strong gold price environment but failing to close the valuation discount to ASX peers due to persistent Burkina Faso geopolitical and security concerns. The company progresses M'pesso studies and exploration without a near-term production catalyst. The stock drifts in line with the gold price, offering leveraged gold exposure but no idiosyncratic re-rating.
supporting: Sanbrado is an established, operating mine with known cost and production parameters, supporting a base case of steady output
Strong gold price environment provides a revenue floor that supports cash generation even without operational upside
Burkina Faso jurisdiction risk is well-understood by the market and likely already embedded in the current discount to NAV
contradicting: Any deterioration in Burkina Faso security could push the stock into the bear case
Absence of current financial data makes it difficult to confirm whether the base case is tracking as expected
M'pesso development timeline uncertainty could frustrate investors expecting near-term growth

hypotheses.title: N3: Risk/Downside
hypotheses.description: Escalating security incidents in Burkina Faso, including jihadist activity in the Sahel region, force WAF to curtail or suspend operations at Sanbrado, triggering a material production miss and potential impairment. The Burkinabe government's increasingly nationalist resource policy, including potential demands for higher state participation or royalty increases, compounds operational risk. This scenario would likely result in a significant de-rating as investors price in stranded asset risk.
supporting: Burkina Faso has experienced significant political instability, including two military coups in 2022, and ongoing Islamist insurgency in the Sahel region
Several international mining companies have faced operational disruptions in the Sahel, establishing a precedent for forced curtailments
The Burkinabe junta has signalled resource nationalism tendencies, creating fiscal and operational uncertainty for foreign miners
WAF's single-asset concentration at Sanbrado means any disruption has an outsized impact on group production and cash flow
contradicting: Sanbrado has continued to operate through prior periods of instability, suggesting some operational resilience
WAF has invested in community relations and local employment, which may provide some social licence buffer
High gold prices improve the economics of maintaining operations even under elevated security costs

hypotheses.title: N4: Disruption/Catalyst
hypotheses.description: A larger gold producer or diversified miner acquires WAF at a significant premium to the prevailing share price, recognising the value of Sanbrado's remaining mine life and the M'pesso growth option in a high gold price environment. Alternatively, WAF executes a transformative merger with another West African operator to create a multi-asset platform with improved capital markets access and reduced single-asset risk. This tail scenario would likely require gold prices to remain elevated and the acquirer to have a higher risk tolerance for Burkina Faso exposure.
supporting: Elevated gold prices historically stimulate M&A activity as majors seek to replace depleting reserves
WAF's discount to NAV relative to peers in safer jurisdictions could make it an attractive acquisition target on a per-ounce basis
Consolidation among West African gold producers has been a recurring theme, with several transactions completed in recent years
contradicting: Burkina Faso jurisdiction risk significantly narrows the pool of potential acquirers willing to take on country risk
Military junta governance creates regulatory uncertainty that complicates deal execution and foreign investment approvals
No current evidence of strategic review, board-level discussions, or market speculation around a transaction

narrative.theNarrative: [Coverage Initiated 2026-03-13 06:06 UTC] West African Resources is an ASX-listed gold producer operating the Sanbrado Gold Mine in Burkina Faso, a jurisdiction that has become increasingly complex for international miners following two military coups in 2022 and an ongoing Islamist insurgency across the broader Sahel region. The dominant narrative for WAF is a tension between exceptional gold price tailwinds and elevated and difficult-to-quantify geopolitical risk -- a combination that has historically resulted in the stock trading at a persistent discount to its net asset value relative to peers operating in safer jurisdictions. The gold price environment as of March 2026 is highly supportive, with silver surging +7.1% in a single session suggesting broad precious metals momentum, and gold itself near multi-year highs that significantly improve Sanbrado's free cash flow generation. The key operational narrative centres on whether WAF can sustain Sanbrado production at nameplate capacity while advancing the M'pesso satellite deposit as the next growth lever, with M'pesso representing a relatively low-capital-intensity path to production growth that could extend the company's mine life profile. The bear case is not an operational one -- Sanbrado is a proven, functioning mine -- but rather a geopolitical one, with the Burkinabe junta's resource nationalism signals and the security situation in the Sahel representing tail risks that are difficult to hedge and that could force a rapid re-pricing of the stock. For investors, WAF offers high leverage to the gold price with a risk profile that demands a higher return threshold than Australian-domiciled peers, and the current gold price environment may be one of the few periods where that risk-reward equation tilts favourably enough to justify the jurisdiction premium.

priceImplication.label: Embedded Assumptions
priceImplication.content:
What the current price likely embeds (indicative, pending price data):

- Continued Sanbrado operations at or near nameplate capacity through the current mine plan, with no material security-related disruption

- A persistent 30-50% discount to NAV relative to Australian-domiciled gold peers, reflecting Burkina Faso jurisdiction risk

- Gold price assumptions likely in the US$2,400-2,600/oz range embedded in consensus, potentially below current spot, suggesting upside if gold holds at current levels

- Low probability assigned to M'pesso development as a near-term production catalyst, with the market treating it as an option rather than a base case

- Minimal M&A premium, consistent with the narrow acquirer pool for Burkina Faso-domiciled assets

- No dividend or capital return premium, consistent with WAF's historical reinvestment-focused capital allocation

narrative.evidenceCheck: The evidence base for this initiation is materially constrained. No financial data, recent ASX announcements, earnings results, or news flow was available at the time of initiation, which significantly limits the precision of our analysis. The commodity context provides one data point -- silver's +7.1% single-session move -- which is directionally supportive for precious metals sentiment but is not company-specific evidence. Our analysis therefore relies heavily on sector knowledge, historical company context, and publicly available information about Burkina Faso's geopolitical environment rather than current financial or operational data. The gaps in corporate communications, financial performance, and operational data are significant and mean that all price targets and probability weightings carry a higher-than-normal uncertainty band. We flag this as a data-limited initiation and note that the analysis should be substantially updated once current production reports, financial results, and company guidance are obtained.
narrative.narrativeStability: The current narrative -- leveraged gold exposure offset by Burkina Faso jurisdiction risk -- is structurally stable in the sense that neither the gold price tailwind nor the geopolitical risk discount is likely to resolve quickly. However, the narrative is vulnerable to binary disruption events: a significant security incident at or near Sanbrado, a junta decree imposing new royalties or state participation requirements, or conversely, a positive operational surprise such as a resource upgrade or M'pesso development decision could each shift the dominant narrative materially. The next test of narrative stability is likely WAF's next quarterly production report, which will confirm whether Sanbrado is tracking to guidance in the current security environment. A miss on production or a cost blowout driven by security-related expenditure would likely accelerate the bear case narrative, while an in-line or beat result would reinforce the base case.

evidence.intro: Evidence assessment pending. Stock was auto-added to coverage on 13 March 2026.

discriminators.intro: The following data points carry high diagnosticity â” they can meaningfully shift probability between competing hypotheses.

rows.evidence: Sanbrado quarterly gold production vs. guidance -- the single most important operational metric that distinguishes between N1 (outperformance), N2 (in-line), and N3 (disruption/miss). Any security-related production curtailment would be immediately visible in this data.
rows.currentReading: Not available at initiation -- Q1 2026 quarterly report pending

rows.evidence: Burkina Faso security incident reports and junta policy announcements -- external data from security monitoring services, news flow, and government decrees that would signal whether the operating environment is stable, deteriorating, or improving. Resource nationalism decrees (royalty increases, state participation demands) would be a direct bear case trigger.
rows.currentReading: Burkina Faso remains under military junta governance with ongoing Sahel insurgency; no specific new policy announcements available at initiation

rows.evidence: Gold spot price trajectory and WAF's realised gold price -- the gold price is the primary revenue driver and a sustained move above US$3,000/oz would materially improve free cash flow generation, potentially shifting weight from N2 to N1. A sharp gold price correction would do the opposite.
rows.currentReading: Gold price near multi-year highs as of March 2026; silver +7.1% in a single session signals strong precious metals momentum

discriminators.nonDiscriminating:
• Gold price sensitivity (in isolation): While gold price is a key revenue driver, all four scenarios assume broadly similar gold price environments -- the discriminating factor is operational and geopolitical, not the gold price itself, which affects all scenarios proportionally.
  • ASX listing and liquidity: WAF's ASX listing and trading liquidity do not discriminate between the bull, base, and bear cases -- they are structural features of the stock rather than scenario-dependent variables.
  • Management tenure and experience: While management quality matters, it does not discriminate between the key hypotheses, which are driven by external factors (gold price, Burkina Faso security) rather than internal execution variables at the margin.
  • Sanbrado mine life (remaining years): The absolute remaining mine life at Sanbrado is a known, slowly-changing variable that does not discriminate between near-term scenarios -- it becomes relevant only in a multi-year horizon analysis.
  • Silver price movements: The +7.1% silver session move is a macro sentiment indicator for precious metals broadly but does not discriminate between WAF-specific hypotheses.

tripwires.intro: The following catalysts could force a material revision to hypothesis weightings. Each carries conditional outcomes that map to specific narrative shifts.

cards.name: Q1 2026 Quarterly Production Report

conditions.if: If Sanbrado produces above 50,000 oz at AISC below US$1,400/oz with no security incidents reported
conditions.then: Then base case (N2) probability increases; gold price leverage is being captured operationally and the jurisdiction risk discount may compress modestly

conditions.if: If production misses guidance materially (below 40,000 oz) or management discloses security-related operational disruptions
conditions.then: Then bear case (N3) probability increases significantly; the market will re-price the probability of a sustained operational disruption

cards.source: WAF ASX quarterly activity report (expected April 2026)

cards.name: FY2026 Half-Year Financial Results

conditions.if: If H1 2026 EBITDA and free cash flow are materially above prior year, driven by gold price tailwinds and stable production
conditions.then: Then bull case (N1) probability increases; the market may begin to close the discount to NAV if cash generation is demonstrably strong

conditions.if: If H1 2026 results show cost inflation, impairments, or reduced guidance for H2 2026
conditions.then: Then bear case (N3) probability increases; investor confidence in the Sanbrado operating model will be tested

cards.source: WAF ASX half-year results announcement (expected August 2026)

cards.name: M'pesso Development Decision / Feasibility Update

conditions.if: If WAF announces a positive feasibility study or development decision for M'pesso with capital costs and timeline within market expectations
conditions.then: Then bull case (N1) probability increases; production growth optionality is converted from an option to a near-term catalyst, potentially triggering a re-rating

conditions.if: If M'pesso studies are deferred, capital costs come in above expectations, or the project is shelved due to security concerns
conditions.then: Then base case (N2) probability decreases and bear case (N3) increases; the growth narrative is undermined and the stock becomes a pure single-asset, declining-reserve story

cards.source: WAF ASX project update announcements (timing indicative)

gaps.analyticalLimitations: This initiation is conducted under severe data constraints, with no current financial data, market data, ASX announcements, or news flow available. All probability weightings, indicative price targets, and scenario descriptions are based on sector knowledge, historical company context, and publicly available information about Burkina Faso's geopolitical environment rather than current company-specific data. The indicative world prices in the position-in-range section are based on qualitative comparable analysis only and should not be treated as formal price targets pending a full NAV model build. The analysis should be substantially refreshed once Q1 2026 production data, current financial results, and management guidance are obtained. Investors should treat this as a framework initiation rather than a fully evidenced investment recommendation.

trend.structure: Price in downtrend relative to 50-day and 200-day moving averages.

footer.disclaimer: This report does not constitute personal financial advice. Continuum Intelligence synthesises cross-domain evidence using the Analysis of Competing Hypotheses (ACH) methodology. All factual claims are sourced from ASX filings, company disclosures, broker consensus data, and publicly available information.

hero.embedded_thesis: Without current price data available at initiation, we cannot precisely characterise what the market is embedding. However, based on WAF's historical trading pattern, the stock has typically traded at a 30-50% discount to NAV relative to Australian-domiciled gold peers, reflecting the Burkina Faso jurisdiction risk premium. At current gold prices near multi-year highs, the market is likely pricing in continued Sanbrado operations at or near guidance, some probability of M'pesso development, but a persistent and material discount for geopolitical risk. The market appears to be assigning a low probability to either a transformative corporate event or a forced operational suspension, with the base case of steady-state production in a strong gold price environment dominating the implied valuation.
hero.skew_description: The four hypotheses sum to 100%, with the base case (N2, 40%) carrying the highest weight as expected for an initial coverage initiation on an established producer. The bear case (N3, 28%) outweighs the bull case (N1, 25%), reflecting the non-trivial and asymmetric downside from Burkina Faso security deterioration. The tail scenario (N4, 7%) captures low-probability M&A optionality in a strong gold price environment.

position_in_range.note: World prices are highly indicative and based on sector comparable analysis only -- pending full W1-W4 NAV valuation build with current production, cost, and reserve data. Current price not available at initiation.

next_decision_point.metric: Sanbrado gold production (oz), All-In Sustaining Cost (AISC, US$/oz), and any security or operational commentary from management
next_decision_point.thresholds: Production above 50,000 oz for the quarter at AISC below US$1,400/oz would support the base case; a miss below 40,000 oz or AISC above US$1,600/oz, or any security incident disclosure, would shift weight toward the bear case
